title: Peloton Reports Q4 2021 Loss, Warns of Lower Q1 Guidance
text: Peloton Q4 2021 Earnings Report. Financial Results: Peloton posted a net loss of $313.2 million, or $1.05 per share, which exceeded the 45-cent loss forecast by analysts. Total revenue grew 54% to $936.9 million, topping estimates for $927.2 million. Stock Impact: Shares were down about 6% in extended trading on the news, after initially falling as much as 15% on the disappointing earnings miss. Pricing Strategy Changes: Peloton warned that its earnings will be hurt in the near term because it's slashing the price of its original Bike machine by about 20%. This aggressive pricing move was intended to boost subscriber growth but signaled margin pressure ahead. Forward Guidance: For its fiscal first quarter, Peloton forecasted sales will reach $800 million, which is well below the $1.01 billion that analysts estimated, indicating significant deceleration expected. Internal Control Issues: The company disclosed it found a problem with the way it has been accounting for inventory, with an audit of fiscal 2021 discovering a material weakness in the internal controls that govern Peloton's financial reporting. This raised governance concerns among investors.
